Name: Distribution point unreachable
Pattern: (?i)failed to get DP locations|failed to resolve (distribution point|DP)|no (valid )?DP (locations|was found)|GetDPLocations failed
Explanation: The client could not locate a distribution point with the requested content. The client may not be in a boundary group with access to a DP, or the DP is offline.
Resolution: Verify the client IP is in a boundary group assigned to a DP. Check that content is distributed to an accessible DP. Test network connectivity to the DP.

Name: Content not found on distribution point
Pattern: (?i)content not found|content.*is not available|no content locations? (found|available)|content location request failed
Explanation: The DP was reachable but did not have the requested content. The content may not be distributed, or distribution may have failed.
Resolution: Check the MECM console for content distribution status. Redistribute if needed. Verify the package/application content source path is accessible.

Name: Content download failure
Pattern: (?i)download failed|content transfer failed|CTM job.*failed|failed to download content|DataTransferService.*error
Explanation: Content was located on a DP but the download failed. Common causes: BITS service issues, network interruption, proxy interference, or DP IIS misconfiguration.
Resolution: Check BITS service status on the client. Verify network connectivity to the DP. Check IIS logs on the DP for HTTP errors. Try redistributing the content.

Name: Insufficient CCM cache space
Pattern: (?i)not enough (available )?space in cache|cache.*is (full|smaller)|insufficient cache|cannot reserve cache
Explanation: The client cache does not have enough free space for the requested content. The content size exceeds available cache capacity.
Resolution: Increase the CCM client cache size in client settings or clear stale cached content. Check disk space on the system drive.

Name: Application not detected after install
Pattern: (?i)application.*not detected after install|detection.*returned false after|post.?install detection.*fail
Explanation: The installer ran successfully but the detection method did not find the application afterward. The detection rule may not match the installed product.
Resolution: Review the detection method. Verify it matches the installed product version, path, or registry key. Check if the app installed to an unexpected location or with a different version string.

Name: Install command execution failed
Pattern: (?i)execution failed|process returned.*non.?zero|install.*returned error|execmgr.*failed to run|failed to (start|execute|run).*install
Explanation: The application install command failed to execute or returned a non-zero exit code. The installer itself encountered an error.
Resolution: Check the application installer log for details. Common causes: missing prerequisites, insufficient permissions, conflicting software, or corrupt installer.

Name: Install exceeded maximum runtime
Explanation: The application install did not complete within the allowed maximum runtime configured on the deployment type. The install may still be running or may have hung.
Resolution: Increase the maximum allowed runtime on the deployment type. Check if the installer is waiting for user interaction or stuck on a prerequisite. Check for pending reboots.

Name: WSUS scan failure
Explanation: The Windows Update Agent failed to complete a software update scan against the WSUS server. The client cannot evaluate update compliance.
Resolution: Verify WSUS server connectivity (port 8530/8531). Check the SoftwareDistribution folder for corruption. Try resetting the WUA components.

Name: WSUS server connection failure
Explanation: The client could not connect to the WSUS server. The server may be down, the app pool may be recycling, or a network issue is blocking the connection.
Resolution: Verify the WSUS server is running and the WsusPool app pool is started in IIS. Check firewall rules for ports 8530/8531. Test connectivity from the client.

Name: No maintenance window for updates
Pattern: (?i)no (current or future )?service window|no maintenance window|outside.*maintenance window|waiting for.*service window
Explanation: The client has pending updates but no maintenance window is currently open or scheduled. Updates will not install until a window opens.
Resolution: Verify a maintenance window exists on the target collection that permits software update installation. Check if the window type is correct (Software Updates vs All Deployments).

Name: BITS transfer failure
Explanation: The Background Intelligent Transfer Service encountered an error during content download. BITS handles all content transfers between client and DP.
Resolution: Restart the BITS service on the client. Clear the BITS queue if stuck jobs exist. Check for proxy or firewall interference with HTTP downloads.

Name: Management point unreachable
Pattern: (?i)failed to (connect|communicate) (to|with) (the )?management point|MP (is )?(not available|unreachable|not responding)|cannot (reach|contact).*management point|ccmsetup.*failed to resolve MP
Explanation: The client could not communicate with its assigned management point. The MP may be down, or a network/firewall issue is blocking the connection.
Resolution: Verify the MP is online and the SMS_MP_CONTROL_MANAGER component is running. Test connectivity on ports 80/443. Check DNS resolution for the MP FQDN.

Name: Client prerequisite missing or failed
Explanation: A required prerequisite for the MECM client was not found or failed to install. Common prerequisites: .NET Framework, Windows Installer, WMI.
Resolution: Manually install the missing prerequisite. Check the ccmsetup.log for the specific prerequisite name and version required.

Name: MOF compile failure (MPP corruption)
Explanation: The Microsoft Policy Platform prerequisite failed during MOF compilation, indicating possible WMI repository corruption on the client.
Resolution: Repair the WMI repository on the client. Run 'winmgmt /salvagerepository' or 'winmgmt /resetrepository' if salvage fails. Then retry client install.

Name: WMI repository corruption
Explanation: WMI errors suggest the WMI repository may be corrupted. Many MECM client functions depend on a healthy WMI repository.
Resolution: Run 'winmgmt /verifyrepository'. If inconsistent, try '/salvagerepository' first, then '/resetrepository' as last resort. A reset requires client reinstall.

Name: Client certificate issue
Explanation: A certificate error was detected. The client PKI certificate may be expired, revoked, or missing. This can block HTTPS communication with site systems.
Resolution: Check the client certificate in the local machine Personal store. Verify the certificate is not expired and the issuing CA is trusted. Re-enroll if needed.

Name: Policy retrieval failure
Explanation: The client failed to download policy from the management point. Without policy, the client cannot evaluate deployments or compliance.
Resolution: Verify management point connectivity. Force a policy refresh cycle on the client. Check if the MP's IIS is responding on the CCM_Incoming virtual directory.

Name: Access denied
Explanation: An access denied error occurred. The client may lack permissions to access a resource, or the machine account may not have the expected rights.
Resolution: Verify the machine account has access to the resource. Check NTFS/share permissions on content sources and DPs. Ensure the client is domain-joined with a valid computer account.

Name: DNS resolution failure
Explanation: A DNS lookup failed. The client cannot resolve the hostname of a site system (MP, DP, or WSUS). This blocks communication with that server.
Resolution: Verify DNS configuration on the client (ipconfig /all). Test name resolution (nslookup <servername>). Check if the DNS record exists and is correct.

Name: Pending reboot blocking operation
Explanation: A pending reboot is preventing an operation from proceeding. A previous install or update requires a restart before new installations can continue.
Resolution: Reboot the client device. If the reboot has been pending for an extended period, investigate what originally required it (Windows Update, software install, etc.).
